Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me produce the detailed TDD implementation plan. --- # TDD Implementation Plan: CardModal Enhancement ## Architecture Overview The plan breaks into 6 subtasks executed in strict order. Each subtask follows the Red-Green-Refactor cycle: write the failing test(s), implement the minimum code to make them pass, then clean up. The 10 existing tests in `CardModal.test.jsx` must continue passing throughout. --- ## Subtask 1 — Board.jsx: selectedCard Freshness (selectedCardId pattern) **Why first**: Every downstream test in Board.test.jsx that touches modal behavior depends on Board passing a fresh card object to CardModal. Fixing this now means all subsequent Board-level tests are written against the correct API. ### 1a. Tests to add to `Board.test.jsx` Add these test cases inside the existing `describe('Board', ...)` block: **Test: "modal reflects updated card title after updateCard resolves"** - Setup: `useBoard` returns state with one card in ready. The mock `updateCard` resolves with a new object that has `title: 'Updated Title'`. - Click the card tile to open the modal. Confirm the title is the original. - Call `updateCard` (simulated by changing what `useBoard` returns via `mockReturnValue` with a new cards state containing the updated card). - Re-render or trigger a state change, then assert `screen.getByRole('dialog')` contains `'Updated Title'`. - **This test will FAIL** under the current `selectedCard` (object) approach because the stored object is stale. **Test: "modal closes when selectedCardId card no longer exists in cards state"** - Setup: `useBoard` returns state with one card. Open the modal. Re-mock `useBoard` to return an empty cards state (card deleted). Re-render. Assert `screen.queryByRole('dialog')` is `null` — the modal auto-closes because `find()` returns `undefined`. ### 1b. Code changes to `Board.jsx` Replace: ``` const [selectedCard, setSelectedCard] = useState(null) ``` with: ``` const [selectedCardId, setSelectedCardId] = useState(null) ``` Build a flat array of all cards for the find: ``` const allCards = [...cards.ready, ...cards.in_progress, ...cards.done] const selectedCard = selectedCardId ? allCards.find(c => c.id === selectedCardId) ?? null : null ``` Change `onCardClick` handler: ``` onCardClick={(card) => setSelectedCardId(card.id)} ``` Change `onClose` handler: ``` onClose={() => setSelectedCardId(null)} ``` The `CardModal` invocation passes `onUpdate` and `onDelete` which come from `useBoard()`: ``` const { cards, loading, error, updateCard, deleteCard } = useBoard() ``` ```jsx {selectedCard && ( setSelectedCardId(null)} onUpdate={updateCard} onDelete={deleteCard} /> )} ``` **Verification**: The two new Board tests pass; all 11 existing Board tests continue to pass. --- ## Subtask 2 — React Portal **Why second**: Portal wrapping is a structural change. It must be in place before testing editing, deletion, and comments — because `createPortal` changes where the DOM nodes live, and tests querying `document.body` need to be written with that in mind. jsdom supports `createPortal` natively: `ReactDOM.createPortal(children, document.body)` renders into `document.body`, so `screen.getByRole('dialog')` still finds the dialog (Testing Library queries the full document by default). ### 2a. Tests to add to `CardModal.test.jsx` **Test: "renders into document.body, not inside the React root"** - `render()` - Get the dialog element: `const dialog = screen.getByRole('dialog')` - Assert `document.body.contains(dialog)` is `true` — this is always true, but the important assertion is: - Assert that the container div created by `render()` (i.e., `document.querySelector('#root') || document.querySelector('[data-testid]')`) does NOT contain the dialog. In practice: assert `dialog.closest('.modal-overlay')` is not null AND that the React render container does not contain `.modal-overlay`. Implementation: `render` returns `{ container }`, then assert `container.contains(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay'))` is `false`. **Test: "portal cleanup — overlay is removed from body after unmount"** - `const { unmount } = render()` - Assert `document.querySelector('.modal-overlay')` is not null. - Call `unmount()`. - Assert `document.querySelector('.modal-overlay')` is `null`. ### 2b. Code changes to `CardModal.jsx` Add import: ```jsx import { createPortal } from 'react-dom' ``` Wrap the return value: ```jsx return createPortal(
e.stopPropagation()} > {/* ... */}
, document.body ) ``` No portal container `useEffect` is needed when targeting `document.body` directly — it always exists in jsdom and in browsers. **Important for existing tests**: The existing test `fireEvent.click(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay'))` will still work because `.modal-overlay` is now in `document.body`, which `querySelector` searches. The test `does not call onClose when modal content area is clicked` also still works. **Verification**: 2 new portal tests pass; all 10 existing tests pass. --- ## Subtask 3 — Editable Title and Assignee with Save/Cancel/Error This is the largest subtask. Break it into two passes (3a: title, 3b: assignee) with a shared save/error mechanism. ### 3a. Tests for editable title Add a new `describe('CardModal — edit title', ...)` block in `CardModal.test.jsx`. Shared fixture for the describe block: ```js const baseCard = { id: '1', title: 'Fix bug', assignee: 'Alice', description: 'Some desc', column: 'ready', position: 1, created_at: 1000, comments: [] } ``` **Test: "shows edit button for title in view mode"** - `render()` - `expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /edit title/i })).toBeInTheDocument()` **Test: "clicking edit title button shows an input with current title value"** - Render. Click the edit-title button. - `expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: /title/i })).toHaveValue('Fix bug')` **Test: "clicking edit title hides the static title h2"** - Render. Click edit-title. - `expect(screen.queryByRole('heading', { level: 2 })).not.toBeInTheDocument()` (or check it's hidden) **Test: "shows Save and Cancel buttons when editing title"** - Render. Click edit-title. - `expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /save/i })).toBeInTheDocument()` - `expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /cancel/i })).toBeInTheDocument()` **Test: "cancel edit title restores original title and hides input"** - Render. Click edit-title. Change input value via `fireEvent.change(input, { target: { value: 'New title' } })`. - Click Cancel. - `expect(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' })).toBeInTheDocument()` - `expect(screen.queryByRole('textbox', { name: /title/i })).not.toBeInTheDocument()` **Test: "Escape key cancels title edit (does not close modal)"** - Render. Click edit-title. - `fireEvent.keyDown(document.body, { key: 'Escape' })` - Assert modal is still in DOM: `expect(screen.getByRole('dialog')).toBeInTheDocument()` - Assert input is gone: `expect(screen.queryByRole('textbox', { name: /title/i })).not.toBeInTheDocument()` - Assert title is restored. **Test: "save title calls onUpdate with card id and new title"** - `const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ ...baseCard, title: 'New title' })` - Render. Click edit-title. Change input value to `'New title'`. Click Save. - `await waitFor(() => expect(onUpdate).toHaveBeenCalledWith('1', { title: 'New title' }))` **Test: "save title shows loading state while onUpdate is pending"** - `const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockReturnValue(new Promise(() => {}))` - Render. Click edit-title. Change value. Click Save. - `expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /saving/i })).toBeDisabled()` (or check the save button has aria-busy / is disabled) **Test: "save title exits edit mode on success"** - `const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ ...baseCard, title: 'New title' })` - Render. Click edit-title. Change value. Click Save. - `await waitFor(() => expect(screen.queryByRole('textbox', { name: /title/i })).not.toBeInTheDocument())` **Test: "save title shows inline error on onUpdate rejection"** - `const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockRejectedValue(new Error('Server error'))` - Render. Click edit-title. Change value. Click Save. - `await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByRole('alert')).toHaveTextContent('Server error'))` **Test: "save title stays in edit mode on onUpdate rejection"** - `const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockRejectedValue(new Error('fail'))` - Render. Click edit-title. Click Save. - `await waitFor(() => expect(onUpdate).toHaveBeenCalled())` - `expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: /title/i })).toBeInTheDocument()` ### 3b. Tests for editable assignee Add `describe('CardModal — edit assignee', ...)` block. Mirror the title tests with these exact equivalents: **Test: "shows edit button for assignee in view mode"** **Test: "clicking edit assignee shows an input with current assignee value"** — `toHaveValue('Alice')` **Test: "clicking edit assignee shows an input with empty string when assignee is null"** — render with `assignee: null`, click edit, `expect(input).toHaveValue('')` **Test: "shows Save and Cancel buttons when editing assignee"** **Test: "cancel edit assignee restores original assignee"** **Test: "Escape key cancels assignee edit (does not close modal)"** **Test: "save assignee calls onUpdate with card id and new assignee"** — `expect(onUpdate).toHaveBeenCalledWith('1', { assignee: 'Bob' })` **Test: "save assignee with empty string calls onUpdate with null assignee"** — clear input, save, `expect(onUpdate).toHaveBeenCalledWith('1', { assignee: null })` **Test: "save assignee shows inline error on rejection"** **Test: "save assignee stays in edit mode on rejection"** ### 3c. Code changes to `CardModal.jsx` Add these new props to the function signature: ```jsx export default function CardModal({ card, onClose, onUpdate, onDelete }) ``` Add these local state declarations inside the component: ```jsx const [isEditingTitle, setIsEditingTitle] = useState(false) const [editTitle, setEditTitle] = useState(card.title) const [isEditingAssignee, setIsEditingAssignee] = useState(false) const [editAssignee, setEditAssignee] = useState(card.assignee ?? '') const [isSaving, setIsSaving] = useState(false) const [saveError, setSaveError] = useState(null) ``` Update the Escape key handler logic: ```jsx useEffect(() => { function onKey(e) { if (e.key === 'Escape') { if (isEditingTitle) { setIsEditingTitle(false) setEditTitle(card.title) setSaveError(null) } else if (isEditingAssignee) { setIsEditingAssignee(false) setEditAssignee(card.assignee ?? '') setSaveError(null) } else { onClose() } } } document.addEventListener('keydown', onKey) return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey) }, [onClose, isEditingTitle, isEditingAssignee, card.title, card.assignee]) ``` Add `handleSaveTitle` and `handleSaveAssignee` async functions: ```jsx async function handleSaveTitle() { setIsSaving(true) setSaveError(null) try { await onUpdate(card.id, { title: editTitle }) setIsEditingTitle(false) } catch (err) { setSaveError(err.message) } finally { setIsSaving(false) } } async function handleSaveAssignee() { setIsSaving(true) setSaveError(null) try { await onUpdate(card.id, { assignee: editAssignee === '' ? null : editAssignee }) setIsEditingAssignee(false) } catch (err) { setSaveError(err.message) } finally { setIsSaving(false) } } ``` JSX structure for title section: ```jsx {isEditingTitle ? (
setEditTitle(e.target.value)} />
) : (

{card.title}

)} ``` Mirror the same pattern for assignee. The error alert goes in the content area once, shared: ```jsx {saveError &&

{saveError}

} ``` **Note on `aria-label` choices**: The Save button aria-label must be distinguishable when both title and assignee are in edit mode simultaneously (the design forbids simultaneous editing since opening edit-title while editing assignee would be confusing, but tests should not allow both open). Use `aria-label="Save title"` and `aria-label="Save assignee"` for specificity if tests need `getByRole('button', { name: /save title/i })`. Alternatively, a single generic `aria-label="Save"` is fine if only one field is ever in edit mode at a time — which the logic enforces. Keep it simple: `aria-label="Save"` and `aria-label="Cancel"`. **Verification**: All ~20 new edit tests pass; all 10 original tests pass; all 2 portal tests pass. --- ## Subtask 4 — Delete with Inline Confirmation ### 4a. Tests to add — new `describe('CardModal — delete', ...)` **Test: "shows a Delete button in view mode"** - `expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /delete/i })).toBeInTheDocument()` **Test: "clicking Delete shows an inline confirmation prompt"** - Click delete button. - `expect(screen.getByText(/are you sure/i)).toBeInTheDocument()` (or similar confirm text) - `expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /confirm delete/i })).toBeInTheDocument()` - `expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /keep card/i })).toBeInTheDocument()` (or "No"/"Cancel") **Test: "clicking keep card (cancel delete) dismisses the confirmation"** - Click delete. Click "Keep card" (cancel). - `expect(screen.queryByText(/are you sure/i)).not.toBeInTheDocument()` - `expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /delete/i })).toBeInTheDocument()` **Test: "clicking Confirm Delete calls onDelete with card id"** - `const onDelete = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined)` - Click delete. Click confirm. - `await waitFor(() => expect(onDelete).toHaveBeenCalledWith('1'))` **Test: "confirm delete button is disabled while onDelete is pending"** - `const onDelete = vi.fn().mockReturnValue(new Promise(() => {}))` - Click delete. Click confirm. - `expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /confirm delete/i })).toBeDisabled()` **Test: "modal calls onClose after successful delete"** - `const onClose = vi.fn()` - `const onDelete = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined)` - Click delete. Click confirm. - `await waitFor(() => expect(onClose).toHaveBeenCalled())` **Test: "shows error message when onDelete rejects"** - `const onDelete = vi.fn().mockRejectedValue(new Error('Delete failed'))` - Click delete. Click confirm. - `await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByRole('alert')).toHaveTextContent('Delete failed'))` **Test: "delete error clears confirmation and returns to view mode"** - `const onDelete = vi.fn().mockRejectedValue(new Error('fail'))` - Click delete. Click confirm. - `await waitFor(() => expect(onDelete).toHaveBeenCalled())` - `expect(screen.queryByText(/are you sure/i)).not.toBeInTheDocument()` ### 4b. Code changes to `CardModal.jsx` Add state: ```jsx const [showDeleteConfirm, setShowDeleteConfirm] = useState(false) const [isDeleting, setIsDeleting] = useState(false) ``` Add handler: ```jsx async function handleConfirmDelete() { setIsDeleting(true) setSaveError(null) try { await onDelete(card.id) onClose() } catch (err) { setSaveError(err.message) setShowDeleteConfirm(false) } finally { setIsDeleting(false) } } ``` JSX for delete section (placed at the bottom of the modal-content, above/alongside the close button): ```jsx {showDeleteConfirm ? (

Are you sure you want to delete this card?

) : ( )} ``` The shared `saveError` `

` renders above this block, so delete errors also appear there. **Verification**: 8 new delete tests pass; all prior tests pass. --- ## Subtask 5 — Comments Section ### 5a. Tests to add — new `describe('CardModal — comments', ...)` Shared fixture: ```js const cardWithComments = { ...baseCard, comments: [ { id: 'cm1', card_id: '1', author: 'Bob', content: 'Looks good!', created_at: 1700000000000 }, { id: 'cm2', card_id: '1', author: 'Alice', content: 'Needs work', created_at: 1700000001000 }, ] } ``` **Test: "renders a comments section heading"** - Render with `cardWithComments`. - `expect(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: /comments/i })).toBeInTheDocument()` **Test: "renders each comment's author"** - `expect(screen.getByText('Bob')).toBeInTheDocument()` - `expect(screen.getByText('Alice')).toBeInTheDocument()` **Test: "renders each comment's content"** - `expect(screen.getByText('Looks good!')).toBeInTheDocument()` - `expect(screen.getByText('Needs work')).toBeInTheDocument()` **Test: "renders each comment's timestamp"** - The display format should be a locale date string. Assert that something formatted from `1700000000000` is visible. Use a regex or extract with `new Date(1700000000000).toLocaleString()`. - `expect(screen.getByText(new Date(1700000000000).toLocaleString())).toBeInTheDocument()` **Test: "renders comments in order (first comment appears before second)"** - Use `screen.getAllByTestId('comment')` (add `data-testid="comment"` to each comment item in the JSX) or use the document order assertion: - `const comments = screen.getAllByTestId('comment')` - `expect(comments[0]).toHaveTextContent('Looks good!')` - `expect(comments[1]).toHaveTextContent('Needs work')` **Test: "renders 'No comments yet' when comments array is empty"** - Render with `{ ...baseCard, comments: [] }`. - `expect(screen.getByText(/no comments yet/i)).toBeInTheDocument()` **Test: "does not render 'No comments yet' when comments exist"** - Render with `cardWithComments`. - `expect(screen.queryByText(/no comments yet/i)).not.toBeInTheDocument()` ### 5b. Code changes to `CardModal.jsx` Add a comments section inside `.modal-content`, below the description: ```jsx

Comments

{card.comments.length === 0 ? (

No comments yet

) : (
    {card.comments.map(cm => (
  • {cm.author} {new Date(cm.created_at).toLocaleString()}

    {cm.content}

    • ))}
)}
``` **Verification**: 7 new comment tests pass; all prior tests pass. --- ## Subtask 6 — CSS: Backdrop Blur, Animations, Responsive This subtask has **no new JS tests** — CSS-only changes are not testable in jsdom. The developer should do a manual visual pass. However, one smoke test can guard against regressions in class names. ### 6a. Smoke test to add (in `CardModal.test.jsx`) **Test: "modal-overlay element has the expected CSS class"** - `render()` - `expect(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay')).toBeInTheDocument()` This test exists implicitly in the existing suite via `fireEvent.click(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay'))` — so no new test is strictly required here. The CSS changes carry no new test obligation. ### 6b. CSS changes to `CardModal.css` Add to `.modal-overlay`: ```css backdrop-filter: blur(4px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(4px); opacity: 0; animation: modal-fade-in 150ms ease forwards; ``` Add keyframes: ```css @keyframes modal-fade-in { to { opacity: 1; } } ``` Add to `.modal-content`: ```css transform: translateY(8px); animation: modal-slide-in 150ms ease forwards; ``` ```css @keyframes modal-slide-in { to { transform: translateY(0); } } ``` Add responsive breakpoint at the bottom: ```css @media (max-width: 600px) { .modal-content { max-width: 100%; width: 100%; min-height: 60vh; border-radius: 12px 12px 0 0; position: fixed; bottom: 0; margin: 0; padding: 1.25rem; } } ``` Add new CSS classes needed by new JSX (edit fields, delete confirm, comments): ```css .modal-field-view { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; } .modal-field-edit { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; flex-wrap: wrap; } .modal-field-edit input { flex: 1; padding: 0.25rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.875rem; border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 4px; } .modal-error { color: #c0392b; font-size: 0.8rem; margin: 0.25rem 0; } .modal-delete { margin-top: 1rem; background: none; border: 1px solid #c0392b; color: #c0392b; padding: 0.3rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 0.8rem; } .modal-delete:hover { background: #c0392b; color: #fff; } .modal-delete-confirm { margin-top: 1rem; padding: 0.75rem; background: #fff5f5; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid #f5c6cb; } .modal-comments { margin-top: 1.25rem; border-top: 1px solid #eee; padding-top: 1rem; } .modal-comments-heading { font-size: 0.9rem; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; color: #444; } .modal-comments-list { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.75rem; } .modal-comment { font-size: 0.8rem; } .modal-comment-meta { display: flex; gap: 0.5rem; color: #777; margin-bottom: 0.2rem; } .modal-comment-author { font-weight: 600; } .modal-comment-time { font-size: 0.75rem; } .modal-comment-content { color: #333; } .modal-no-comments { font-size: 0.8rem; color: #999; font-style: italic; } ``` --- ## Full Test Execution Order (TDD Cycle per Subtask) ``` Subtask 1: Board.test.jsx — 2 new tests (freshness) RED: write tests → FAIL GREEN: refactor Board.jsx to use selectedCardId PASS: 2 new + 11 existing Board tests pass Subtask 2: CardModal.test.jsx — 2 new portal tests RED: write tests → FAIL GREEN: add createPortal to CardModal.jsx PASS: 2 new + 10 existing CardModal tests pass Subtask 3: CardModal.test.jsx — ~21 new edit tests (title + assignee) RED: write tests → FAIL GREEN: add edit state + JSX + handlers PASS: 21 new + 12 existing CardModal tests pass Subtask 4: CardModal.test.jsx — 8 new delete tests RED: write tests → FAIL GREEN: add showDeleteConfirm state + JSX + handler PASS: 8 new + 33 existing tests pass Subtask 5: CardModal.test.jsx — 7 new comment tests RED: write tests → FAIL GREEN: add comments section JSX PASS: 7 new + 41 existing tests pass Subtask 6: CSS only — no new tests Update CardModal.css with blur, animations, responsive, new classes Existing test suite still passes (48 total tests) ``` --- ## Handling the Portal in jsdom `ReactDOM.createPortal(children, document.body)` works in jsdom because: 1. `document.body` exists in jsdom from the very first render. 2. `@testing-library/react`'s `render()` appends a `div` to `document.body` as the React root, but the portal targets `document.body` directly — so portal content is a sibling of the React root div, not a child. 3. `screen.getByRole('dialog')` uses `document.body` as its search root by default, so it finds the portal content. 4. The test for portal isolation uses `container` (the React root div returned by `render()`) to assert `container.contains(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay'))` is `false`. 5. No special setup is needed in `test-setup.js`. **Gotcha to document**: Because the portal renders into `document.body`, if multiple tests run without proper cleanup, `.modal-overlay` elements can accumulate. `@testing-library/react` calls `cleanup()` automatically after each test (it registers an `afterEach` in its setup). This removes the React root `div` from `document.body`, which also unmounts the portal content. This is safe. --- ## Key Decisions Summary **selectedCardId vs selectedCard**: `setSelectedCardId(card.id)` in `onCardClick`, then `allCards.find(c => c.id === selectedCardId)` derives the fresh card on every render. When `updateCard` resolves and `useBoard` updates its state, `Board` re-renders with the new `cards`, `allCards` rebuilds, and `selectedCard` automatically points to the updated object. When `deleteCard` resolves, the card disappears from `cards`, `find()` returns `undefined`, `selectedCard` becomes `null`, and the modal unmounts. **`onUpdate` and `onDelete` as props**: `CardModal` accepts `onUpdate(id, data)` and `onDelete(id)` as injected dependencies. `Board` passes `updateCard` and `deleteCard` from `useBoard()`. This keeps `CardModal` pure and independently testable. **Edit mode mutual exclusivity**: The implementation can start both `isEditingTitle` and `isEditingAssignee` independently, but clicking one edit button while the other is active is an edge case the tests do not cover. The simplest safe approach: clicking "Edit title" while `isEditingAssignee` is `true` should first cancel the assignee edit (reset `isEditingAssignee` to `false`, restore `editAssignee`). This can be enforced in the `onClick` handlers and does not require a separate test (it's an untested UX nicety, not a behavioral requirement in the spec). **`isSaving` shared flag**: A single `isSaving` flag covers both title and assignee saves. Since only one field is ever in edit-mode at a time (or should be), this is safe. The delete operation uses `isDeleting` separately to avoid conflating save-error with delete-error. --- ### Critical Files for Implementation - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-11/kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-11/kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-11/kanban/client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-11/kanban/client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-11/kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.css`

content.text:
Here's a summary of the implementation plan:

## CardModal Enhancement Plan

### What exists already
A basic read-only `CardModal.jsx` lives in `client/src/components/Board/` with 10 passing tests. It renders a plain overlay without React Portal, and has no edit/delete/comments functionality.

### 6 Subtasks in TDD order

**1. Board.jsx — selectedCardId freshness** (2 new tests)
Store only `selectedCardId` (string) in Board state; derive the live card via `allCards.find(c => c.id === selectedCardId)`. This means after `updateCard` resolves and `useBoard` refreshes cards, the modal automatically shows the latest data. Also wire up `onUpdate={updateCard}` and `onDelete={deleteCard}` props to CardModal.

**2. React Portal** (2 new tests)
Wrap the modal return in `createPortal(..., document.body)`. The portal test verifies `container.contains('.modal-overlay')` is `false` while `document.body.contains(...)` is `true`. All existing tests keep working since RTL queries the full document.

**3. Editable title + assignee fields** (~20 new tests)
Local `isEditingTitle`/`isEditingAssignee` state, controlled inputs, async `handleSaveTitle`/`handleSaveAssignee` calling `onUpdate`. Escape while editing cancels the edit (not close). Error alert renders on rejection; save stays in edit mode. Assignee empty string saved as `null`.

**4. Delete with inline confirmation** (8 new tests)
"Delete" button → shows inline "Are you sure?" prompt → "Confirm delete" calls `onDelete(id)` then `onClose()`. "Keep card" dismisses the prompt. Errors shown in shared alert.

**5. Comments section** (7 new tests)
Renders `card.comments` as a list with author, content, and `toLocaleString()` timestamp. "No comments yet" shown for empty array.

**6. CSS polish** (no new tests)
Backdrop blur (`backdrop-filter: blur(4px)`), fade-in + slide-in animations, mobile bottom-sheet at ≤600px, and all new element classes for edit fields, delete confirm, and comments.

**Total**: ~39 new tests, all 21 existing tests must stay green throughout.

Also wire up `onUpdate={updateCard}` and `onDelete={deleteCard}` props to CardModal.

**2. React Portal** (2 new tests)
Wrap the modal return in `createPortal(..., document.body)`. The portal test verifies `container.contains('.modal-overlay')` is `false` while `document.body.contains(...)` is `true`. All existing tests keep working since RTL queries the full document.

**3. Editable title + assignee fields** (~20 new tests)
Local `isEditingTitle`/`isEditingAssignee` state, controlled inputs, async `handleSaveTitle`/`handleSaveAssignee` calling `onUpdate`. Escape while editing cancels the edit (not close). Error alert renders on rejection; save stays in edit mode. Assignee empty string saved as `null`.

**4. Delete with inline confirmation** (8 new tests)
"Delete" button → shows inline "Are you sure?" prompt → "Confirm delete" calls `onDelete(id)` then `onClose()`. "Keep card" dismisses the prompt. Errors shown in shared alert.

**5. Comments section** (7 new tests)
Renders `card.comments` as a list with author, content, and `toLocaleString()` timestamp. "No comments yet" shown for empty array.

**6. CSS polish** (no new tests)
Backdrop blur (`backdrop-filter: blur(4px)`), fade-in + slide-in animations, mobile bottom-sheet at ≤600px, and all new element classes for edit fields, delete confirm, and comments.

**Total**: ~39 new tests, all 21 existing tests must stay green throughout.

Now let me carefully review the plan for issues. Based on my analysis, I've identified these critical issues:

1. **CRITICAL: Missing title validation** — The task spec explicitly says "form validation for required fields" and "validation prevents empty title". The plan has no test or implementation for this.

2. **CRITICAL: Existing CardModal test fixtures not updated** — All 10 existing tests render `<CardModal card={card} onClose={vi.fn()} />` without `onUpdate`/`onDelete`. After enhancement, the component renders Edit/Delete buttons that call these. While the existing tests won't trigger those paths, the plan should explicitly say to update these fixtures to avoid confusion and fragility.
   
3. **CRITICAL: Edit mode mutual exclusivity not implemented** — The plan treats mutual exclusivity between `isEditingTitle` and `isEditingAssignee` as a "UX nicety" but doesn't implement it, yet allowing both states simultaneously creates a broken UI where both edit fields appear at once.

4. **CRITICAL: Enter key for save in edit mode missing** — Subtask 3 explicitly requires "Enter key for save actions", but the plan marks this as optional when it's actually a stated requirement.

5. **Important: Board.test.jsx freshness tests lack** — The thought cuts off here, but I'm checking what's missing from the Board test coverage.

6. **Important: `isSaving` state creates logic errors** — Since both edit modes could be active simultaneously, both save buttons could end up disabled at the same time, which is a cascading problem from issue 3.

Actually, issues 3 and 6 are interconnected—enforcing mutual exclusivity for the edit modes automatically solves the `isSaving` conflict since only one save operation could be in progress. For the Enter key requirement, I could attach an `onKeyDown` handler directly to the input elements rather than using a document-level listener, which feels more natural for inline editing.

Writing input handlers... Writing test cases... The new buttons won't interfere with the existing close button test since the regex `/close/i` specifically targets "Close" and won't match "Delete", "Edit title", or "Edit assignee". For the dialog click test, I need to think through whether clicking the dialog element itself triggers the overlay's click handler or if the `stopPropagation` on the content div prevents that.

Actually, when the test clicks the dialog element directly, the `stopPropagation` on the modal content prevents the event from bubbling up to the overlay, so `onClose` won't be called. The existing tests should pass without updating fixtures, though it's good practice to add `onUpdate={vi.fn()}` and `onDelete={vi.fn()}` to the test fixtures.

For the edit mode mutual exclusivity, I'm setting up the click handler for "Edit title" to enable title editing mode, populate the edit field with the current title, and cancel any active assignee editing by resetting those state values.

The same pattern applies for "Edit assignee" — clicking it enables assignee editing, resets the title editing state, and clears any save errors. This ensures only one field can be edited at a time.

I'm adding tests to verify that opening title edit cancels assignee edit and vice versa.

For the Enter key handling...

Still writing input handlers...

For title validation, I'm checking that the title isn't empty before saving, showing an error message if it is, and preventing the update call when validation fails.

Now I'm thinking through the shared `isSaving` state — since mutual exclusivity is enforced, only one field can be in edit mode at a time, so only one save handler runs concurrently. The shared flag should work fine.

But I need to consider what happens if `handleSaveTitle` is running while the user tries to switch to editing the assignee.

Actually, with mutual exclusivity, the user can't be in both edit modes simultaneously in the normal UI flow. Once `isSaving` is true, the save buttons are disabled anyway, and only one save button is visible at a time. So there's no real race condition issue here.

Let me now finalize the plan with all these considerations. I need to add explicit test implementations for Board.test.jsx using the rerender pattern, update existing CardModal test renders to include the missing props, enforce edit mode mutual exclusivity in both implementation and tests, add title validation logic and tests, implement Enter key handling for saving, and clarify that the shared `isSaving` flag is safe given the mutual exclusivity constraint.

1 # Plan: CardModal Enhancement (Task #11) 2 3 ## Context 4 5 The project is a Kanban board (React + Vitest). A basic `CardModal` already exists at `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` — it is read-only and renders a plain overlay (no portal). This task enhances it with: React Portal rendering, editable title/assignee fields, save/cancel/error handling, delete with confirmation, comments section display, and visual polish (backdrop blur, animations, responsive CSS). 6 7 The task dependency (#10) is complete. All existing tests (10 CardModal, 11 Board) must keep passing throughout. 8 9 --- 10 11 ## Critical Files 12 13 | File | Change Type | 14 |---|---| 15 | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` | Major enhancement | 16 | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | ~40 new tests | 17 | `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` | selectedCardId pattern + pass new props | 18 | `client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` | 2 new tests for card freshness | 19 | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.css` | New classes + blur/animation | 20 21 --- 22 23 ## Subtask 1 — Board.jsx: selectedCardId freshness pattern 24 25 **Problem**: `Board` stores `selectedCard` as a card *object*. After `updateCard` resolves, `useBoard` updates its `cards` state but `selectedCard` stays stale. Fix: store only the card ID; derive the live card from `cards` state each render. 26 27 ### Tests first (`Board.test.jsx`) — RED 28 29 ```js 30 it('modal reflects updated card data when useBoard cards state changes', () => { 31 const card = { ...MOCK_CARD, title: 'Old Title' } 32 useBoard.mockReturnValue({ 33 ...DEFAULT_STATE, 34 cards: { ready: [card], in_progress: [], done: [] }, 35 }) 36 const { rerender } = render() 37 fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Old Title' })) 38 expect(screen.getByRole('dialog')).toHaveTextContent('Old Title') 39 40 // Simulate useBoard updating the card in place (e.g. after updateCard resolves) 41 useBoard.mockReturnValue({ 42 ...DEFAULT_STATE, 43 cards: { ready: [{ ...card, title: 'New Title' }], in_progress: [], done: [] }, 44 }) 45 rerender() 46 expect(screen.getByRole('dialog')).toHaveTextContent('New Title') 47 }) 48 49 it('modal closes automatically when selected card is removed from cards state', () => { 50 useBoard.mockReturnValue({ 51 ...DEFAULT_STATE, 52 cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] }, 53 }) 54 const { rerender } = render() 55 fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' })) 56 expect(screen.getByRole('dialog')).toBeInTheDocument() 57 58 // Simulate card being deleted from state 59 useBoard.mockReturnValue({ 60 ...DEFAULT_STATE, 61 cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, 62 }) 63 rerender() 64 expect(screen.queryByRole('dialog')).toBeNull() 65 }) 66 ``` 67 68 ### Implementation (`Board.jsx`) 69 70 Replace: 71 ```jsx 72 const { cards, loading, error } = useBoard() 73 const [selectedCard, setSelectedCard] = useState(null) 74 ``` 75 with: 76 ```jsx 77 const { cards, loading, error, updateCard, deleteCard } = useBoard() 78 const [selectedCardId, setSelectedCardId] = useState(null) 79 const allCards = [...cards.ready, ...cards.in_progress, ...cards.done] 80 const selectedCard = selectedCardId ? (allCards.find(c => c.id === selectedCardId) ?? null) : null 81 ``` 82 83 Change handlers: 84 ```jsx 85 // onCardClick in each Column: 86 onCardClick={(card) => setSelectedCardId(card.id)} 87 88 // CardModal invocation: 89 {selectedCard && ( 90 setSelectedCardId(null)} 93 onUpdate={updateCard} 94 onDelete={deleteCard} 95 /> 96 )} 97 ``` 98 99 --- 100 101 ## Subtask 2 — React Portal 102 103 **Tests first (`CardModal.test.jsx`) — RED** 104 105 ```js 106 it('renders modal outside the React root container (via portal)', () => { 107 const { container } = render() 108 // Portal renders into document.body, NOT inside the React render container 109 expect(container.contains(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay'))).toBe(false) 110 expect(document.body.contains(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay'))).toBe(true) 111 }) 112 113 it('cleans up portal content from document.body on unmount', () => { 114 const { unmount } = render() 115 expect(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay')).not.toBeNull() 116 unmount() 117 expect(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay')).toBeNull() 118 }) 119 ``` 120 121 **Note on existing tests**: All 10 existing CardModal tests render with `` (no `onUpdate`/`onDelete`). After the enhancement these props are required for Edit/Delete interactions. The existing tests do not click those buttons, so they will continue to pass — but **all existing `render(...)` calls in `CardModal.test.jsx` must be updated** to include `onUpdate={vi.fn()} onDelete={vi.fn()}` to make the test suite robust and consistent with the new interface. 122 123 **Note on portal discoverability**: `screen.getByRole` searches `document.body` by default, so all existing role-based queries keep working after the portal change. 124 125 ### Implementation (`CardModal.jsx`) 126 127 ```jsx 128 import { createPortal } from 'react-dom' 129 // ... 130 return createPortal( 131
132
e.stopPropagation()}> 134 {/* existing content */} 135
136
, 137 document.body 138 ) 139 ``` 140 141 New props in signature: `{ card, onClose, onUpdate, onDelete }` 142 143 --- 144 145 ## Subtask 3 — Editable Title and Assignee Fields 146 147 ### New state in `CardModal.jsx` 148 149 ```jsx 150 const [isEditingTitle, setIsEditingTitle] = useState(false) 151 const [editTitle, setEditTitle] = useState(card.title) 152 const [isEditingAssignee, setIsEditingAssignee] = useState(false) 153 const [editAssignee, setEditAssignee] = useState(card.assignee ?? '') 154 const [isSaving, setIsSaving] = useState(false) 155 const [saveError, setSaveError] = useState(null) 156 ``` 157 158 **Edit mode mutual exclusivity**: Only one field may be in edit mode at a time. Opening one edit field must close the other. This is enforced in the `onClick` handlers (see JSX below). Because mutual exclusivity is enforced, the shared `isSaving` flag is safe — at most one save can be in flight at a time. 159 160 ### Escape key handler update 161 162 When editing, Escape cancels the edit (does NOT close modal): 163 ```jsx 164 useEffect(() => { 165 function onKey(e) { 166 if (e.key !== 'Escape') return 167 if (isEditingTitle) { 168 setIsEditingTitle(false); setEditTitle(card.title); setSaveError(null) 169 } else if (isEditingAssignee) { 170 setIsEditingAssignee(false); setEditAssignee(card.assignee ?? ''); setSaveError(null) 171 } else { 172 onClose() 173 } 174 } 175 document.addEventListener('keydown', onKey) 176 return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey) 177 }, [onClose, isEditingTitle, isEditingAssignee, card.title, card.assignee]) 178 ``` 179 180 ### Save handlers 181 182 ```jsx 183 async function handleSaveTitle() { 184 // Validation: title is required 185 if (editTitle.trim() === '') { 186 setSaveError('Title is required') 187 return 188 } 189 setIsSaving(true); setSaveError(null) 190 try { 191 await onUpdate(card.id, { title: editTitle }) 192 setIsEditingTitle(false) 193 } catch (err) { setSaveError(err.message) } 194 finally { setIsSaving(false) } 195 } 196 197 async function handleSaveAssignee() { 198 setIsSaving(true); setSaveError(null) 199 try { 200 await onUpdate(card.id, { assignee: editAssignee === '' ? null : editAssignee }) 201 setIsEditingAssignee(false) 202 } catch (err) { setSaveError(err.message) } 203 finally { setIsSaving(false) } 204 } 205 ``` 206 207 ### JSX structure for title field 208 209 ```jsx 210 {isEditingTitle ? ( 211
212 setEditTitle(e.target.value)} 216 onKeyDown={e => { if (e.key === 'Enter') handleSaveTitle() }} 217 /> 218 221 225

content.text:
The plan now addresses all five issues:

1. **Title validation** — `handleSaveTitle` guards against empty trimmed string with `setSaveError('Title is required')` and returns early (no API call). Test added: `"Save shows validation error and does not call onUpdate when title is empty"`.

2. **Existing test fixtures** — Explicit instruction to update all 10 existing `render(...)` calls in `CardModal.test.jsx` to include `onUpdate={vi.fn()} onDelete={vi.fn()}`.

3. **Edit mode mutual exclusivity** — Each "Edit" button's `onClick` explicitly resets the *other* field's edit state back to closed before opening its own. Tests added: `"opening title edit cancels any open assignee edit"` and `"opening assignee edit cancels any open title edit"`. The shared `isSaving` flag is documented as safe because of this invariant.

4. **Enter key for save** — Added `onKeyDown={e => { if (e.key === 'Enter') handleSave...() }}` on each input (not a global listener), with tests `"Enter key in title input triggers save"` and `"Enter key in assignee input triggers save"`.

5. **Board freshness tests** — Replaced pseudocode stubs with complete test implementations using RTL's `rerender()` pattern: mock initial state → click card → verify dialog → change mock → `rerender(<Board />)` → verify updated content.

prompt:
Carefully evaluate your plan, in order to identify:
- missing tasks or subtasks, as well as their testing strategies
- technical flaws in the plan (architecture flaws, logic flaws, inconsistencies, overlooked aspects)
- UX and usability flaws (missing parts, inconsistent UI, lack of clarity)
- missing details, ambiguity, inconsistencies and lack of clarity about what will be implemented

The plan will be implemented by a LLM, so it should be accurate and provide all details necessary for a correct execution.

Once you have finished your evaluation, update the plan accordingly.

Only flag issues that would cause the implementation to be **incorrect or incomplete relative to the task requirements**. Do NOT nitpick minor style improvements. The goal is a correct and sufficient plan, not a perfect one.

If there is nothing to update, just tell `<nothing_updated />`.

content.thinking:
Let me carefully re-read the plan and the task requirements to identify any remaining issues that would cause incorrect or incomplete implementation.

Let me re-read the task requirements first, then the plan.

Task requirements:
- Create modal with React Portal
- Editable title and assignee fields
- Description display area
- Comments section
- Save/cancel for edits
- Delete card button with confirmation
- Keyboard shortcuts (Escape to close)
- Style with backdrop blur and centered positioning

Subtask details:
1. Modal structure with React Portal - portal implementation, open/close state management
2. Editable fields with save/cancel - controlled components, edit mode toggle, form validation, save/cancel, loading states, error handling


3. Delete confirmation and keyboard shortcuts - delete with confirmation, Escape key, Enter key for save, focus management
4. Comments section display - display existing comments with author/timestamps, CommentList or basic display, empty state
5. Modal styling and backdrop effects - backdrop blur, center positioning, z-index, animations, responsive

Now let me carefully read the plan to spot issues:

**Issue 1: The plan doesn't explicitly mention the `description` display area**

The task says "Include editable title and assignee fields, description display area". The description is mentioned in the existing `CardModal.jsx` as a read-only display. The task specifies "description display area" — this is a display-only field, not editable, so it should be included in the modal but doesn't need edit mode logic.

The plan shows the new JSX structure for title and assignee fields but doesn't explicitly place the description display in the updated structure. A developer implementing this could easily forget to include it, which is a gap in the plan.

**Issue 2: The plan has TWO "Save" buttons with the same aria-label="Save"**

In the title edit JSX there's a save button, and in the assignee edit JSX there's another save button with the same aria-label. Due to mutual exclusivity, only one renders at a time, but this creates ambiguity in tests — the test for "Save calls onUpdate(id, { title: newTitle })" would need to distinguish which save button is being tested.

**Issue 3: The plan doesn't mention updating `aria-label` on the dialog after title edits**

The dialog's aria-label is set to the card title, which updates automatically when the card prop changes after a successful save. But during the edit itself, the aria-label still reflects the old title while the user is typing a new one, creating a mismatch between what the dialog announces and what's actually being edited.

**Issue 4: The Save title test needs proper async handling**

The plan mentions adding an Enter key handler to the title input, but the test implementation needs to account for the fact that `handleSaveTitle` is async and triggers an API call. I'd need to mock the `onUpdate` callback and use `waitFor` to verify the async behavior completes, which the plan doesn't explicitly cover.

**Issue 5: Loading state test requires async utilities**

Testing the disabled button and "Saving…" message while the request is pending...

Actually, I need to reconsider this. When `handleSaveTitle` is called, `isSaving(true)` happens synchronously before the await, and React processes state updates synchronously in test mode by default, so checking the button state immediately after the click should work without needing `act` or `waitFor`. But I should verify the test properly asserts the disabled state and loading message appear during the pending request.

**Issue 6: Verifying the loading state assertion**

The test needs to confirm that when `handleSaveTitle` is awaiting the promise, `isSaving` is true and the button shows the "Saving…" text and is disabled—the plan should be explicit about checking these assertions while the request is in flight.

**Issue 7: Validation error handling**

When `editTitle` is empty and Save is clicked, the code should call `setSaveError('Title is required')` without calling `onUpdate` or exiting edit mode. The test needs to verify the error message appears and `onUpdate` is not invoked.

To test this properly, I need to clear the input field after clicking "Edit title" — setting its value to an empty string — then click Save and assert the error displays. The plan covers the key assertions, though the implementation would need to handle that clearing step explicitly.

**Issue 8:**

The state initialization with `useState(card.title)` and `useState(card.assignee ?? '')` only happens once on mount, but since `selectedCard` is derived from the `cards` state, the `card` prop updates when `updateCard` succeeds. The plan handles this by resetting `editTitle` when entering edit mode and in cancel handlers, so the editing state stays synced with the current card. There's a minor edge case where external prop changes during editing could leave `editTitle` stale, but more critically, after a successful save the `card` prop updates and I need to verify the state resets properly.

When the user re-enters edit mode, the onClick handler resets `editTitle` to the new `card.title`, so this works correctly. For the assignee field, when saving an empty assignee value, the API call sends `null` and the `card.assignee` updates accordingly, but I need to trace through whether the edit state properly reflects this change. The plan doesn't show the complete JSX structure for CardModal, which could lead developers to accidentally omit the description display area—a key requirement. Additionally, the `saveError` state is shared across title saves, assignee edits, and delete operations, so clearing it in one context (like opening the edit title field) could mask errors from other operations like failed deletions.

The `isSaving` flag is also shared between title and assignee save buttons, but since mutual exclusivity prevents both edit modes from being active simultaneously, this shouldn't cause issues in practice. The plan mentions this mutual exclusivity but doesn't explicitly call out how it protects the shared `isSaving` state.

The two rejection tests serve different purposes—one verifies the error alert appears while the other confirms edit mode stays active—so both are necessary despite the similar test names.

The description field is completely missing from the plan's new JSX snippets, even though the task explicitly requires it. The existing description display needs to be preserved in the enhanced modal body.

Now I'm thinking through the "modal reflects updated card data" test and whether it'll work after the portal change. Since `selectedCardId` lives in Board's local state rather than in the mock, it should persist across rerenders. When the test rerenders with updated card data, `allCards.find()` will locate the new card and pass it to CardModal. However, there's a potential timing issue—the test was written before the portal change, so the modal's DOM location might affect how the test finds and verifies the updated data.

For the Enter key handler, using `onKeyDown` with a direct inline check for the Enter key is straightforward and will work fine with `fireEvent.keyDown()` in tests, even though `handleSaveTitle` isn't wrapped in useCallback.

The test structure mentions describe blocks for "edit title" and "edit assignee" but only lists them as bullet points without the actual test implementations.

The async tests for saving—calling `onUpdate`, exiting edit mode on success, and showing error alerts—all need `await waitFor()` to properly handle the promise resolution, which the plan doesn't explicitly call out.

Now I'm checking what imports the Board test file will need...

the `rerender` function comes from destructuring the `render` return value, not as a separate import, so the existing imports from `@testing-library/react` should cover it. But I notice the plan doesn't address how to test the Escape key behavior when the edit form is rendered in a portal...

Looking at the existing test, pressing Escape calls `onClose`, which works because the edit flags are false initially. The new test for canceling a title edit also fires Escape on `document.body`, and since the handler checks `isEditingTitle` first, it should prevent the modal from closing. That should work correctly.

Let me focus on what's actually critical here: the missing description display area is the main gap I need to address. I'm checking the async test patterns across the codebase — useBoard.test.js has extensive `waitFor` usage, but CardModal.test.jsx doesn't have async tests yet, so I should explicitly call out that developers need to use `await waitFor(...)` for save/delete operations. Combined with making sure the description element is clearly preserved in the JSX structure, these are the two concrete fixes the plan needs.

This task enhances it with: React Portal rendering, editable title/assignee fields, save/cancel/error handling, delete with confirmation, comments section display, and visual polish (backdrop blur, animations, responsive CSS). 6 7 The task dependency (#10) is complete. After `updateCard` resolves, `useBoard` updates its `cards` state but `selectedCard` stays stale. **Async test pattern**: Any test that asserts on the outcome of `onUpdate` or `onDelete` (which are async) must use `await waitFor(...)`. Example:
```js
const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ ...card, title: 'New title' })
// ... render, click edit, change input, click Save ...
await waitFor(() => expect(onUpdate).toHaveBeenCalledWith('1', { title: 'New title' }))
```
Import `waitFor` from `@testing-library/react`. Tests checking the *loading* state (isSaving while promise is pending) do not need `waitFor` — they check synchronous state after `fireEvent.click`.

1 # Plan: CardModal Enhancement (Task #11) 2 3 ## Context 4 5 The project is a Kanban board (React + Vitest). A basic `CardModal` already exists at `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` — it is read-only and renders a plain overlay (no portal). This task enhances it with: React Portal rendering, editable title/assignee fields, save/cancel/error handling, delete with confirmation, comments section display, and visual polish (backdrop blur, animations, responsive CSS). 6 7 The task dependency (#10) is complete. All existing tests (10 CardModal, 11 Board) must keep passing throughout. 8 9 --- 10 11 ## Critical Files 12 13 | File | Change Type | 14 |---|---| 15 | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` | Major enhancement | 16 | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | ~40 new tests | 17 | `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` | selectedCardId pattern + pass new props | 18 | `client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` | 2 new tests for card freshness | 19 | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.css` | New classes + blur/animation | 20 21 --- 22 23 ## Subtask 1 — Board.jsx: selectedCardId freshness pattern 24 25 **Problem**: `Board` stores `selectedCard` as a card *object*. After `updateCard` resolves, `useBoard` updates its `cards` state but `selectedCard` stays stale. Fix: store only the card ID; derive the live card from `cards` state each render. 26 27 ### Tests first (`Board.test.jsx`) — RED 28 29 ```js 30 it('modal reflects updated card data when useBoard cards state changes', () => { 31 const card = { ...MOCK_CARD, title: 'Old Title' } 32 useBoard.mockReturnValue({ 33 ...DEFAULT_STATE, 34 cards: { ready: [card], in_progress: [], done: [] }, 35 }) 36 const { rerender } = render() 37 fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Old Title' })) 38 expect(screen.getByRole('dialog')).toHaveTextContent('Old Title') 39 40 // Simulate useBoard updating the card in place (e.g. after updateCard resolves) 41 useBoard.mockReturnValue({ 42 ...DEFAULT_STATE, 43 cards: { ready: [{ ...card, title: 'New Title' }], in_progress: [], done: [] }, 44 }) 45 rerender() 46 expect(screen.getByRole('dialog')).toHaveTextContent('New Title') 47 }) 48 49 it('modal closes automatically when selected card is removed from cards state', () => { 50 useBoard.mockReturnValue({ 51 ...DEFAULT_STATE, 52 cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] }, 53 }) 54 const { rerender } = render() 55 fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' })) 56 expect(screen.getByRole('dialog')).toBeInTheDocument() 57 58 // Simulate card being deleted from state 59 useBoard.mockReturnValue({ 60 ...DEFAULT_STATE, 61 cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, 62 }) 63 rerender() 64 expect(screen.queryByRole('dialog')).toBeNull() 65 }) 66 ``` 67 68 ### Implementation (`Board.jsx`) 69 70 Replace: 71 ```jsx 72 const { cards, loading, error } = useBoard() 73 const [selectedCard, setSelectedCard] = useState(null) 74 ``` 75 with: 76 ```jsx 77 const { cards, loading, error, updateCard, deleteCard } = useBoard() 78 const [selectedCardId, setSelectedCardId] = useState(null) 79 const allCards = [...cards.ready, ...cards.in_progress, ...cards.done] 80 const selectedCard = selectedCardId ? (allCards.find(c => c.id === selectedCardId) ?? null) : null 81 ``` 82 83 Change handlers: 84 ```jsx 85 // onCardClick in each Column: 86 onCardClick={(card) => setSelectedCardId(card.id)} 87 88 // CardModal invocation: 89 {selectedCard && ( 90 setSelectedCardId(null)} 93 onUpdate={updateCard} 94 onDelete={deleteCard} 95 /> 96 )} 97 ``` 98 99 --- 100 101 ## Subtask 2 — React Portal 102 103 **Tests first (`CardModal.test.jsx`) — RED** 104 105 ```js 106 it('renders modal outside the React root container (via portal)', () => { 107 const { container } = render() 108 // Portal renders into document.body, NOT inside the React render container 109 expect(container.contains(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay'))).toBe(false) 110 expect(document.body.contains(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay'))).toBe(true) 111 }) 112 113 it('cleans up portal content from document.body on unmount', () => { 114 const { unmount } = render() 115 expect(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay')).not.toBeNull() 116 unmount() 117 expect(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay')).toBeNull() 118 }) 119 ``` 120 121 **Note on existing tests**: All 10 existing CardModal tests render with `` (no `onUpdate`/`onDelete`). After the enhancement these props are required for Edit/Delete interactions. The existing tests do not click those buttons, so they will continue to pass — but **all existing `render(...)` calls in `CardModal.test.jsx` must be updated** to include `onUpdate={vi.fn()} onDelete={vi.fn()}` to make the test suite robust and consistent with the new interface. 122 123 **Note on portal discoverability**: `screen.getByRole` searches `document.body` by default, so all existing role-based queries keep working after the portal change. 124 125 ### Implementation (`CardModal.jsx`) 126 127 ```jsx 128 import { createPortal } from 'react-dom' 129 // ... 130 return createPortal( 131
132
e.stopPropagation()}> 134 {/* existing content */} 135
136
, 137 document.body 138 ) 139 ``` 140 141 New props in signature: `{ card, onClose, onUpdate, onDelete }` 142 143 --- 144 145 ## Subtask 3 — Editable Title and Assignee Fields 146 147 ### New state in `CardModal.jsx` 148 149 ```jsx 150 const [isEditingTitle, setIsEditingTitle] = useState(false) 151 const [editTitle, setEditTitle] = useState(card.title) 152 const [isEditingAssignee, setIsEditingAssignee] = useState(false) 153 const [editAssignee, setEditAssignee] = useState(card.assignee ?? '') 154 const [isSaving, setIsSaving] = useState(false) 155 const [saveError, setSaveError] = useState(null) 156 ``` 157 158 **Edit mode mutual exclusivity**: Only one field may be in edit mode at a time. Opening one edit field must close the other. This is enforced in the `onClick` handlers (see JSX below). Because mutual exclusivity is enforced, the shared `isSaving` flag is safe — at most one save can be in flight at a time. 159 160 ### Escape key handler update 161 162 When editing, Escape cancels the edit (does NOT close modal): 163 ```jsx 164 useEffect(() => { 165 function onKey(e) { 166 if (e.key !== 'Escape') return 167 if (isEditingTitle) { 168 setIsEditingTitle(false); setEditTitle(card.title); setSaveError(null) 169 } else if (isEditingAssignee) { 170 setIsEditingAssignee(false); setEditAssignee(card.assignee ?? ''); setSaveError(null) 171 } else { 172 onClose() 173 } 174 } 175 document.addEventListener('keydown', onKey) 176 return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey) 177 }, [onClose, isEditingTitle, isEditingAssignee, card.title, card.assignee]) 178 ``` 179 180 ### Save handlers 181 182 ```jsx 183 async function handleSaveTitle() { 184 // Validation: title is required 185 if (editTitle.trim() === '') { 186 setSaveError('Title is required') 187 return 188 } 189 setIsSaving(true); setSaveError(null) 190 try { 191 await onUpdate(card.id, { title: editTitle }) 192 setIsEditingTitle(false) 193 } catch (err) { setSaveError(err.message) } 194 finally { setIsSaving(false) } 195 } 196 197 async function handleSaveAssignee() { 198 setIsSaving(true); setSaveError(null) 199 try { 200 await onUpdate(card.id, { assignee: editAssignee === '' ? null : editAssignee }) 201 setIsEditingAssignee(false) 202 } catch (err) { setSaveError(err.message) } 203 finally { setIsSaving(false) } 204 } 205 ``` 206 207 ### JSX structure for title field 208 209 ```jsx 210 {isEditingTitle ? ( 211

lines: ## Subtask 3 — Editable Title and Assignee Fields
+Add to the `react` import: `useState`, `useRef` (in addition to the existing `useEffect`).
+**Focus management**: When edit mode is entered, focus must move to the corresponding input automatically. Implement with two effects:
+Attach the refs to the inputs (see JSX below): `ref={titleInputRef}` on the title input and `ref={assigneeInputRef}` on the assignee input.

**Edit mode mutual exclusivity**: Only one field may be in edit mode at a time. Opening one edit field must close the other. This is enforced in the `onClick` handlers (see JSX below). Because mutual exclusivity is enforced, the shared `isSaving` flag is safe — at most one save can be in flight at a time.

Let me carefully evaluate the plan one more time. I need to be extremely thorough and check every aspect of it.

## Going through each section:

### Subtask 1 — Board.jsx Tests

The Board tests use `rerender`. Looking at the Board.test.jsx:
- Imports: `render, screen, fireEvent, within` — does NOT include `rerender`. But `rerender` comes from destructuring `render()`, not as a separate import. The test does `const { rerender } = render(<Board />)`. This is correct - `rerender` is returned by `render()`, not imported separately.


- The existing test file already has `within` imported, and the new tests don't require any additional imports since `render`, `screen`, and `fireEvent` are already there. ✓

### Subtask 1 — Board.jsx Implementation

Setting up the component state with the board hook, tracking the selected card, and merging all cards from different status columns...

### Subtask 2 — Portal Tests

The portal test verifies that the modal overlay gets rendered into the DOM by checking if the container includes the modal element. Since each test gets a fresh render and cleanup runs between tests, there should be exactly one modal overlay present. ✓

### Subtask 3 — State and Refs

Adding `useState` and `useRef` to the React imports alongside the existing hooks. The component now has three `useEffect` calls total: the original escape key handler, plus two new focus effects for the title and assignee inputs. ✓

Setting up the focus effects for both input fields... The save handler captures `editTitle` from the closure, so it always has the current value when called. For the Enter key, pressing it on the input triggers the `onKeyDown` handler to save, but since the inputs aren't in a form, there's no risk of a double save from an accidental button click.

When the "Edit title" button is clicked, it batches multiple state updates together—setting `isEditingTitle` to true, resetting `editTitle` to the card's original title, turning off assignee editing, and clearing any errors. Once React re-renders with these changes, the focus effect kicks in and puts the cursor in the title input field.

For the save and cancel buttons, both edit sections use the same aria-labels since only one is visible at a time due to mutual exclusivity, so test queries like `getByRole('button', { name: /save/i })` will correctly find exactly one button. The close button in the modal has its own distinct `aria-label="Close"`, so it won't interfere with the edit button tests.

Looking at the heading structure, the modal now contains an h2 for the title and an h3 for the comments section. The existing test that checks for the card title text will still pass since `toHaveTextContent` does a substring match, and "Fix bug" appears in the dialog content even with all the other elements like edit buttons and assignee info.

For the test that checks if clicking the edit title button hides the static heading...

Checking heading roles...

The `<h3>` Comments heading is a real heading element, so `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: /comments/i })` will find it. ✓

Looking at the fixture setup - when using `card` with empty comments, the modal shows "No comments yet" but the tests don't verify this, which is fine. ✓

The semantic structure looks good: close button, h2 title, assignee field, description, error alert, then the h3 Comments section, and finally the delete section. ✓

Now checking what happens when someone tries to save an empty title - pressing Enter with blank `editTitle` triggers `handleSaveTitle()`, which validates that the title isn't empty and sets an error message if it is.  The `onUpdate` callback doesn't fire, edit mode stays open, and the error displays. ✓

The Board tests are set up correctly with the `beforeEach` establishing a default mock, then individual tests override it as needed before rendering. When a rerender happens, it uses whatever mock was set most recently. ✓

For the delete flow, `onDelete` receives the current `card.id` from the closure since the card prop stays in sync with `selectedCardId`. After `onDelete` resolves and `onClose` is called, `selectedCardId` becomes null and the modal unmounts. The optimistic update in `deleteCard` removes the card from state immediately, so `selectedCard` becomes null before the modal even closes.

But here's the issue: `handleConfirmDelete` is async, and while it's awaiting `onDelete`, the optimistic update triggers a re-render that unmounts the component. When the async function tries to call `onClose()` after the await completes, the component is already gone—calling callbacks on an unmounted component could cause problems.

In the tests though, `onDelete` is mocked to resolve asynchronously, but the Board's state doesn't actually change from the mock. So the modal stays mounted, `onClose()` gets called properly, and the test passes. In the real app, the optimistic update would close the modal before `onClose()` even fires, which is fine—it's just a UX quirk where the modal disappears immediately rather than waiting for the async operation.

The `isDeleting` flag works as expected: it's set to true before awaiting the delete, which disables the button, and then reset in the finally block. The test with a never-resolving promise confirms the button stays disabled while the operation is pending.

Now I'm looking at how `saveError` is being reused for both save and delete operations, and the plan is to clear it at the start of each handler. The `<h3>` heading has the accessible name "Comments" which the test can find, but technically the `<section>` should have `aria-labelledby` pointing to that heading to be a proper landmark region—though that's more of an accessibility enhancement than a requirement for what we're testing.

The CSS class `.modal-comments-heading` matches the className applied to the `<h3>`, so styling will work correctly. Now I need to check how the existing escape key test behaves after these changes.

The test currently renders CardModal with just `onClose`, but the plan requires updating all renders to include `onUpdate` and `onDelete` handlers. Once those are added, the escape handler still works the same way—when neither title nor assignee is being edited, it calls `onClose()`. 

For the Board tests, after moving the dialog to render in the document body via a portal, `getByRole('dialog')` will still find exactly one dialog since only one CardModal renders at a time.

Now looking at the mutual exclusivity behavior: when "Edit title" is clicked while the assignee field is active, React batches the state updates together. This sets `isEditingTitle` to true and `isEditingAssignee` to false in the same render cycle. The focus effects then run—the title input gets focus because its condition is now true, while the assignee input doesn't because its condition is false.

Writing the test assertion... the focus effect should have run. ✓

Actually, I need to reconsider. In React 18, there's a distinction between synchronous and concurrent rendering. RTL uses `act()` which flushes microtasks and effects. With `useEffect` (not `useLayoutEffect`), effects run after paint. In jsdom with `act()`, effects should be flushed before `act()` returns. So `fireEvent.click` (which wraps in `act()`) should flush the focus effect. ✓

### POTENTIAL ISSUE: The focus test uses `toHaveFocus()`

`toHaveFocus()` from jest-dom checks that `document.activeElement === element`. For this to work, the input must actually receive focus. Since `titleInputRef.current.focus()` is called in a `useEffect`, and RTL's `act()` flushes effects, this should work. ✓

But there's one edge case: in some jsdom environments, `focus()` might not actually set `document.activeElement` properly, especially if the element isn't visible or isn't in the DOM tree correctly.

### POTENTIAL ISSUE: `screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: /title/i })`

The title input has `aria-label="Title"`, so `getByRole('textbox', { name: /title/i })` should find it since the accessible name matches. Same logic applies to the assignee input with `aria-label="Assignee"`. ✓

Now I'm checking the fixture data in the test file to see what the `card` object contains, particularly the `created_at` field...

The fixture uses `Date.now()` for `created_at`, which gets evaluated when the module loads. For the comments test, `cardWithComments` spreads the base card and adds comments with fixed timestamps, so that's consistent. ✓

I've systematically verified all the key functionality: the Portal rendering, edit mode toggling for title and assignee, save and cancel with validation, error handling, delete confirmation, keyboard shortcuts (Escape and Enter), focus management with refs, preventing simultaneous edits, comments display, description preservation, and the backdrop. Everything checks out.

Now I'm double-checking the Board test where the modal reflects updated card data. When I click the card button with 'Old Title', the modal opens and the dialog is found. The CardModal adds more buttons and content, but `screen.getByRole('dialog')` still locates the single dialog correctly. The text content assertions pass for both the initial 'Old Title' and the updated 'New Title' after rerender. ✓

Since the portal renders the modal into `document.body`, the `screen.getByRole('dialog')` query still finds it even though this is a Board test—the dialog is a child of Board but rendered at the document root. ✓

Now I'm checking for CSS duplication. The existing `CardModal.css` has the base `.modal-overlay` styles, and the plan adds backdrop-filter properties...

Adding blur effects to the overlay...

Then there's the animation property. Multiple `.modal-overlay` blocks will cascade correctly, so all properties apply. ✓

But I'm noticing a potential issue with the mobile styles—the `.modal-content` is set to `position: fixed` inside a `.modal-overlay` that's already `position: fixed`, which breaks the stacking context and positions it relative to the viewport instead of staying within the overlay.

This actually works for a bottom sheet pattern, but it means the flex centering from the overlay no longer applies to the content. The positioning relies entirely on `bottom: 0`. That's intentional, so it's fine.

Now I need to check if there's anything else that could cause incomplete implementation. The plan doesn't mention that the `useEffect` dependency for the Escape handler already exists in the component and needs to be updated.

Updating the Escape key handler... I see the real risk here—if an LLM adds a second Escape handler instead of replacing the existing one, the test would fail because `onClose` would be called twice. The plan should explicitly say to replace the existing handler, not add a new one alongside it. I'm also double-checking the other details: the CSS styling for the modal heading is already covered, and `useState` is already imported in Board.jsx, so no issues there. I need to revise the plan to clarify that the Escape key `useEffect` should replace the existing one in CardModal, not be added alongside it, since running both would trigger `onClose` twice and break the test assertion.

content.text: One genuine issue remains: the plan presents the updated Escape key `useEffect` under "Escape key handler update" but never explicitly states that it **replaces** the existing `useEffect` in `CardModal.jsx`. If an LLM adds it as a second handler instead of replacing the original, there will be two `document` `keydown` listeners in view mode — pressing Escape would call `onClose` twice, and the existing test `'calls onClose when Escape key is pressed'` (which asserts `toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)`) would fail.

content.thinking:
The test `renders each comment author` uses `screen.getByText('Alice')`, which finds multiple elements because 'Alice' appears in both the assignee field (`card.assignee === 'Alice'`) and as a comment author in the `cardWithComments` fixture. 

The `cardWithComments` fixture has `assignee: 'Alice'` (from spreading `...card`) and `comments[1].author === 'Alice'`.

I need to fix the test to use a more specific query. Since the comment author is in a `<span class="modal-comment-author">`, I can use `within` to scope the query, or use `getAllByText` and check length, or use `within` on the comments list.


I think the cleanest approach is to use `getAllByText('Alice')` to get all matching elements and verify that at least one is a comment author, or I could scope the query to the comments section if it has a queryable element. Looking at the fixture, the `cardWithComments` has multiple comments including one with author 'Bob', so I should adjust the test to target a comment author that appears only once. Scoping the query to the comments list with `within()` is the cleanest approach here, since I can target just the `<ul>` element and avoid the ambiguity with Alice appearing as both assignee and comment author.

content.text:
All 6 subtasks are complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

---

**Subtask 1 — Board.jsx selectedCardId pattern** (`Board.jsx` + 2 new tests)
- Board now stores `selectedCardId` (string) instead of the card object
- `selectedCard` is derived each render via `allCards.find(c => c.id === selectedCardId)`, so the modal always shows live data
- `updateCard` and `deleteCard` are destructured from `useBoard()` and passed as `onUpdate`/`onDelete` to CardModal

**Subtask 2 — React Portal** (`CardModal.jsx` + 2 new tests)
- Modal renders via `createPortal(..., document.body)` — outside the React root container
- All existing 10 tests updated to pass `onUpdate={vi.fn()} onDelete={vi.fn()}`

**Subtask 3 — Editable fields** (15 title tests + 13 assignee tests)
- Editable title and assignee with controlled inputs, `useRef`-based auto-focus, `useEffect` focus effects
- `handleSaveTitle` validates non-empty title; both handlers show loading state and inline error on rejection
- Escape key cancels edit (not close); Enter key triggers save; mutual exclusivity enforced

**Subtask 4 — Delete with confirmation** (8 tests)
- Inline confirmation dialog; `handleConfirmDelete` calls `onDelete(card.id)` then `onClose()`
- Shared `saveError` state shows errors from both save and delete operations

**Subtask 5 — Comments section** (7 tests)
- Comments list with author, `toLocaleString()` timestamp, content; "No comments yet" empty state
- `data-testid="comment"` on each `<li>` for order testing

**Subtask 6 — CSS polish** (no new tests)
- Backdrop blur, fade-in/slide-in animations, `max-height: 90vh` scroll, mobile bottom-sheet at ≤600px, all new element classes

**178 tests passing, 0 failing.**
