content.text:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Implement Task #11 "Create CardModal component for card details" following strict TDD (write tests first, then implementation, make all tests pass before moving on)
   - Work exclusively in the git worktree at `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-11`
   - Follow the detailed plan stored at `/Users/benjamin/.claude/plans/snappy-waddling-spring.md` which went through 5 rounds of evaluation and refinement
   - Mark subtask statuses done via `update-subtask-status <id> done --repo /Users/benjamin/personal-git/unsheetify-factory`
   - Evaluate the final implementation against all requirements and fix any issues

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - React 18 with Vitest + @testing-library/react for testing
   - `ReactDOM.createPortal(..., document.body)` for modal rendering outside the app root
   - `useRef` + `useEffect` for focus management on edit inputs
   - `selectedCardId` pattern in Board: store only the card ID in state, derive the live card object from `useBoard`'s `cards` state each render to avoid stale references after `updateCard`
   - Edit mode mutual exclusivity: opening one field's edit mode closes the other
   - Shared `isSaving` flag safe because of mutual exclusivity
   - Single `useEffect` Escape key handler (replacing the original, not adding a second one) with branching for edit-mode vs view-mode behavior
   - `await waitFor(...)` required for async operation outcome assertions in tests
   - `within()` from RTL for scoping queries to avoid ambiguous matches (e.g., 'Alice' appearing in both assignee field and comment author)
   - Optimistic update pattern in `useBoard` hook (pre-existing, not changed)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx`** (modified)
     - Changed `selectedCard` (card object) to `selectedCardId` (string ID) + derived `selectedCard`
     - Destructures `updateCard` and `deleteCard` from `useBoard()`
     - Passes `onUpdate={updateCard}` and `onDelete={deleteCard}` to CardModal
     ```jsx
     const { cards, loading, error, updateCard, deleteCard } = useBoard()
     const [selectedCardId, setSelectedCardId] = useState(null)
     const allCards = [...cards.ready, ...cards.in_progress, ...cards.done]
     const selectedCard = selectedCardId ? (allCards.find(c => c.id === selectedCardId) ?? null) : null
     // ...
     onCardClick={(card) => setSelectedCardId(card.id)}
     // ...
     {selectedCard && (
       <CardModal
         card={selectedCard}
         onClose={() => setSelectedCardId(null)}
         onUpdate={updateCard}
         onDelete={deleteCard}
       />
     )}
     ```

   - **`client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx`** (completely rewritten)
     - Imports: `useEffect, useState, useRef` from 'react'; `createPortal` from 'react-dom'
     - Props: `{ card, onClose, onUpdate, onDelete }`
     - State: `isEditingTitle`, `editTitle`, `isEditingAssignee`, `editAssignee`, `isSaving`, `saveError`, `showDeleteConfirm`, `isDeleting`
     - Refs: `titleInputRef`, `assigneeInputRef`
     - Two focus `useEffect`s firing when edit mode booleans become true
     - Single Escape key `useEffect` with `[onClose, isEditingTitle, isEditingAssignee, card.title, card.assignee]` deps, replacing original
     - `handleSaveTitle` with empty-title validation (`setSaveError('Title is required')`)
     - `handleSaveAssignee` converting empty string to `null`
     - `handleConfirmDelete` calling `onDelete(card.id)` then `onClose()`
     - Wrapped in `createPortal(..., document.body)`
     - Content order: Close button → Title (editable) → Assignee (editable) → Description (read-only) → Error alert → Comments section → Delete/confirmation block
     - Comments section: `<section>` with `<h3>`, list with `data-testid="comment"` on each `<li>`, `toLocaleString()` timestamps

   - **`client/src/components/Board/CardModal.css`** (enhanced)
     - Added `backdrop-filter: blur(4px)` and `-webkit-backdrop-filter` to `.modal-overlay`
     - Added `animation: modal-fade-in 150ms ease` and `@keyframes modal-fade-in`
     - Added `animation: modal-slide-in 150ms ease`, `max-height: 90vh`, `overflow-y: auto` to `.modal-content` plus `@keyframes modal-slide-in`
     - Added all new CSS classes: `.modal-field-view`, `.modal-field-edit`, `.modal-field-edit input`, `.modal-error`, `.modal-delete`, `.modal-delete:hover`, `.modal-delete-confirm`, `.modal-comments`, `.modal-comments-heading`, `.modal-comments-list`, `.modal-comment`, `.modal-comment-meta`, `.modal-comment-author`, `.modal-comment-time`, `.modal-comment-content`, `.modal-no-comments`
     - Added `@media (max-width: 600px)` mobile bottom sheet

   - **`client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx`** (completely rewritten)
     - Added `waitFor` and `within` to imports from `@testing-library/react`
     - All 10 existing tests updated: `render(<CardModal card={card} onClose={vi.fn()} onUpdate={vi.fn()} onDelete={vi.fn()} />)`
     - 2 portal tests (`describes('CardModal — portal', ...)`)
     - 15 title edit tests (`describe('CardModal — edit title', ...)`)
     - 13 assignee edit tests (`describe('CardModal — edit assignee', ...)`)
     - 8 delete tests (`describe('CardModal — delete', ...)`)
     - 7 comments tests (`describe('CardModal — comments', ...)`) with `cardWithComments = { ...card, comments: [...] }`

   - **`client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx`** (2 tests added)
     - `'modal reflects updated card data when useBoard cards state changes'`: uses `rerender(<Board />)` after changing mock return value
     - `'modal closes automatically when selected card is removed from cards state'`: same pattern with empty cards

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`npm test` failing with "vitest: command not found"**: Dependencies weren't installed. Fixed by running `npm install` in the client directory.
   - **Test "renders each comment author" failing** with "Found multiple elements with the text: Alice": The `cardWithComments` fixture spreads `...card` which has `assignee: 'Alice'`, so 'Alice' appeared in both the assignee paragraph and as a comment author. Fixed by importing `within` from RTL and scoping the query: `within(screen.getByRole('list')).getByText('Alice')` and `within(screen.getByRole('list')).getByText('Bob')`.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Solved stale `selectedCard` problem by storing only `selectedCardId` in Board state and deriving the live card object from `useBoard`'s `cards` state each render
   - Solved double Escape handler problem by explicitly replacing (not supplementing) the original `useEffect`
   - Solved focus management by using `useRef` + `useEffect` that fires when `isEditing*` booleans change
   - Solved mutual exclusivity by resetting the other field's state in each "Edit" button's `onClick`
   - Solved ambiguous `getByText('Alice')` query by using `within(screen.getByRole('list'))` scoping

6. All user messages:
   - Initial task: create a detailed implementation plan for Task #11 (with full task JSON)
   - "Carefully evaluate your plan..." (5 separate evaluation requests, each asking for plan updates)
   - "Continue from where you left off." (4 times, after each ExitPlanMode call)
   - "Implement the following plan. Follow TDD..." (with full plan text, worktree path, subtask IDs)
   - "Evaluate implementation" (current message — text-only analysis requested)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Current evaluation task: verify implementation against requirements and fix any issues found
   - No other explicit pending tasks

8. Current Work:
   The implementation is complete. All 178 tests pass across 7 test files:
   - 2 new Board tests (selectedCardId freshness) ✓
   - 2 portal tests ✓
   - 15 title edit tests ✓
   - 13 assignee edit tests ✓
   - 8 delete confirmation tests ✓
   - 7 comments section tests ✓
   - All 10 original CardModal tests still passing ✓
   - All 11 original Board tests still passing ✓
   
   The evaluation request asks to check requirements against implementation, run lint/tests, check for bugs, and fix any issues. Since this is a text-only response, no tools can be called.

   Based on my analysis of the conversation, the implementation is correct and complete:
   - React Portal via `createPortal` ✓
   - Editable title with validation, save/cancel, loading state, error handling ✓
   - Editable assignee with save/cancel, loading state, error handling ✓
   - Description display area preserved ✓
   - Comments section with author/timestamp/content and empty state ✓
   - Delete with inline confirmation dialog ✓
   - Escape key: cancels edit when editing, closes modal when viewing ✓
   - Enter key triggers save on inputs ✓
   - Focus management via useRef/useEffect ✓
   - Backdrop blur + animations + responsive CSS ✓
   - selectedCardId pattern keeps modal data fresh ✓
   - Subtask statuses updated via `update-subtask-status` ✓

9. Optional Next Step:
   The evaluation is complete. The implementation satisfies all requirements from the plan. All 178 tests pass. The user's most recent request was: "Evaluate implementation... Check the requirements against the implementation... Fix all the issues you have identified." Based on the analysis, no issues were found that require fixing — the implementation is complete and correct.
